examples.title: Simple Hypervisor Function Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates how a hypervisor allocates resources to virtual machines
examples.code:
FUNCTION AllocateResources(vm_id, cpu_cores, memory_mb)
BEGIN
  IF CheckAvailability(cpu_cores, memory_mb) THEN
    FOR each vm IN ActiveVMs
      IF vm.id == vm_id THEN
        vm.cpu_cores = cpu_cores
        vm.memory = memory_mb
        RETURN "Resources allocated successfully"
      END IF
    END FOR
    RETURN "VM not found"
  ELSE
    RETURN "Insufficient resources available"
  END IF
END

FUNCTION CheckAvailability(required_cpu, required_mem)
BEGIN
  total_cpu = GetTotalCPU()
  total_mem = GetTotalMemory()
  used_cpu = GetUsedCPU()
  used_mem = GetUsedMemory()
  
  available_cpu = total_cpu - used_cpu
  available_mem = total_mem - used_mem
  
  RETURN (available_cpu >= required_cpu AND available_mem >= required_mem)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode simulates basic hypervisor functions of checking resource availability and allocating CPU cores and memory to virtual machines.

examples.title: Client-Server Request Handler
examples.description: Demonstrates simple client-server communication in computing environment
examples.code:
import socket
import threading

def handle_client(client_socket, client_id):
    print(f"Client {client_id} connected")
    try:
        request = client_socket.recv(1024).decode('utf-8')
        print(f"Request from Client {client_id}: {request}")
        
        # Process request and prepare response
        response = f"Server processed: {request}"
        client_socket.send(response.encode('utf-8'))
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}")
    finally:
        client_socket.close()
        print(f"Client {client_id} disconnected")

def start_server(host='localhost', port=9999):
    server = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
    server.bind((host, port))
    server.listen(5)
    print(f"Server listening on {host}:{port}")
    
    client_id = 1
    while True:
        client_socket, addr = server.accept()
        print(f"Accepted connection from {addr}")
        
        # Create new thread for each client
        client_thread = threading.Thread(
            target=handle_client, 
            args=(client_socket, client_id)
        )
        client_thread.start()
        client_id += 1

if __name__ == "__main__":
    start_server()
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates a basic TCP server handling multiple clients using threading, representing client-server computing environment.

examples.title: Real-Time Task Scheduling Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates priority-based scheduling in RTOS environment
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates priority-based task scheduling in real-time systems, where higher priority tasks are executed first.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n log n) for sorting, O(n) for execution

examples.title: Load Balancer for Distributed Environment
examples.description: Demonstrates load distribution across multiple servers in distributed computing
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LoadBalancer
BEGIN
  servers = INITIALIZE_SERVER_LIST()
  
  FUNCTION RouteRequest(request)
  BEGIN
    // Select server with least current connections
    selected_server = servers[0]
    min_connections = selected_server.active_connections
    
    FOR i = 1 TO servers.length - 1
      IF servers[i].active_connections < min_connections THEN
        selected_server = servers[i]
        min_connections = servers[i].active_connections
      END IF
    END FOR
    
    // Forward request to selected server
    selected_server.active_connections = selected_server.active_connections + 1
    response = FORWARD_TO_SERVER(selected_server, request)
    selected_server.active_connections = selected_server.active_connections - 1
    
    RETURN response
  END FUNCTION
  
  FUNCTION HealthCheck()
  BEGIN
    FOR each server IN servers
      IF NOT server.is_responsive THEN
        REMOVE_SERVER(server)
        LOG("Server " + server.id + " removed from pool")
      END IF
    END FOR
  END FUNCTION
  
  RETURN RouteRequest, HealthCheck
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm demonstrates load balancing in distributed environments, distributing client requests across multiple servers based on least connections.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for selecting server, O(n) for health check
